name: Walter of Aquitaine
description: Walter of Aquitaine is a legendary hero of Germanic epic poetry, particularly known from the Latin epic poem *Waltharius*. He is a Visigothic prince who, along with his betrothed Hildegund and her companion Hagen, escapes from the clutches of Attila the Hun. Walter's story is one of unwavering loyalty, extraordinary martial prowess, and cunning strategy. He is renowned for his incredible strength and resilience, demonstrated in his legendary battle against King Gunther of Burgundy and his twelve champions. The epic recounts their journey, fraught with danger and challenges, as they strive to return to Walter's homeland. Walter's character embodies the virtues of bravery, fidelity, and resourcefulness, making him a compelling figure in Germanic heroic tradition. He represents the ideal of a noble warrior who overcomes seemingly insurmountable odds through courage and skill. His tale serves as a testament to the enduring power of loyalty and the pursuit of freedom. Walter's actions and decisions highlight the importance of honor and self-reliance in a world filled with treachery and conflict. He stands as a symbol of resistance against oppression and a beacon of hope for those seeking liberation. His story is a cornerstone of medieval Germanic literature, inspiring generations with its themes of heroism and perseverance.
subtitle: Visigothic Prince, Unwavering Hero
achievements: Defeating King Gunther and his champions

keyMyths.title: Escape from Attila
keyMyths.description: Walter, Hildegund, and Hagen escape from Attila's court, carrying with them treasures and facing numerous dangers on their journey. This escape sets the stage for the epic confrontation with Gunther. The story emphasizes their resourcefulness and determination to regain their freedom.

keyMyths.title: Battle with Gunther
keyMyths.description: Walter engages in a brutal battle with King Gunther and his twelve champions, ultimately defeating them but suffering grievous wounds himself. This fight showcases Walter's incredible strength and fighting skills. It's a key scene demonstrating his heroic nature.

keyMyths.title: Return to Aquitaine
keyMyths.description: After the grueling battle, Walter, Hildegund, and Hagen finally reach Aquitaine, completing their long and perilous journey. This marks the successful culmination of their quest for freedom and home. Their safe return signifies the triumph of loyalty and courage.

sources.author: Marion Gibbs
sources.title: Waltharius: Text and Translation
sources.description: A comprehensive edition of the *Waltharius* epic poem, providing valuable insights into the story and its historical context.

sources.author: D.H. Green
sources.title: The Carolingian Lord: Semantic Studies in Four Old High German Words
sources.description: Explores the cultural and social context of the *Waltharius* poem, shedding light on the values and beliefs of the time.

associations.name: Sword
associations.significance: Represents Walter's martial prowess and his ability to defend himself and his companions.

associations.name: Loyalty
associations.significance: Walter's unwavering commitment to Hildegund and Hagen, even in the face of adversity.

associations.name: Forest
associations.significance: Symbolizes the wilderness and the challenges Walter faces during his escape and journey.

associations.name: Hildegund
associations.significance: Represents love, fidelity, and the reason for Walter's perilous journey.

associations.name: Hagen
associations.significance: Embodies friendship, camaraderie, and the bonds that are tested during times of hardship.

associations.name: Wolf
associations.significance: Reflects Walter's cunning, resilience, and ability to survive in the wild.

cross_cultural_parallels.similarity: Both Odysseus and Walter are renowned for their cunning and strategic thinking in overcoming obstacles.
Both are legendary warriors known for their incredible strength and martial skills in battle.
Both Sigurd and Walter are heroic figures who face dangerous challenges and demonstrate unwavering courage.

worshipPractices.name: Heroic Ballads
worshipPractices.description: While not directly worshipped, Walter's deeds were celebrated through ballads and epic poems, preserving his legacy and inspiring audiences with his heroic qualities.

cultural.modernLegacy: Walter of Aquitaine continues to be a symbol of courage, loyalty, and resilience in Germanic folklore and literature, influencing modern interpretations of heroic archetypes and inspiring contemporary works of fiction.

extendedContent.title: Early Life and Captivity
extendedContent.content: Walter's early life is shrouded in legend, but he is generally depicted as a Visigothic prince, a member of a noble family destined for greatness. However, fate took an unexpected turn when he, along with Hildegund, a Burgundian princess, and Hagen, a Frankish warrior, were taken as hostages by Attila the Hun. Raised in Attila's court, Walter distinguished himself through his exceptional martial skills and unwavering loyalty. Despite being held captive, he maintained his noble bearing and secretly planned an escape, waiting for the opportune moment to reclaim his freedom and fulfill his destiny. This period of captivity serves as a crucible, forging Walter's character and setting the stage for his later heroic exploits. The bonds formed during this time, particularly with Hildegund and Hagen, would prove crucial in the trials to come, highlighting the importance of loyalty and friendship in the face of adversity.

extendedContent.title: Greatest Quests and Battles
extendedContent.content: Walter's most renowned quest is his daring escape from Attila's court, accompanied by Hildegund and Hagen. Laden with treasure, they embark on a perilous journey back to their homelands, facing numerous challenges and adversaries along the way. Walter's extraordinary martial prowess is put to the test as he single-handedly defeats entire armies, demonstrating his unmatched skill in combat. One of the most famous episodes is his battle against Gunther, King of the Franks, and his warriors, where Walter, despite being severely wounded, manages to fend off their attacks and protect his companions. His cunning strategy and unwavering determination are key to his survival, showcasing his ability to outwit his enemies and overcome seemingly insurmountable odds. These battles serve as a testament to Walter's heroic stature and his unwavering commitment to safeguarding those under his protection.

extendedContent.title: Legacy and Influence
extendedContent.content: Walter of Aquitaine's legacy extends far beyond the tales of his heroic exploits. He represents the ideals of courage, loyalty, and resilience, inspiring generations with his unwavering commitment to justice and freedom. His story has been immortalized in various literary works, most notably the Latin epic poem *Waltharius*, which cemented his place in Germanic folklore. Walter's character continues to resonate in modern interpretations of heroic archetypes, influencing contemporary works of fiction and inspiring individuals to overcome adversity with strength and determination. His tale serves as a reminder of the enduring power of the human spirit and the importance of upholding moral principles in the face of overwhelming challenges. Walter of Aquitaine remains a timeless symbol of heroism, forever etched in the annals of Germanic legend.

shortDescription: Walter of Aquitaine is a Germanic hero known for his escape from Attila with Hildegund & Hagen. His tale embodies loyalty, martial skill, & cunning strategy.
